macro_segments.title: Introduction to Sechirut: Choker vs. Kablan and Restrictions on Shatla (Planter)

micro_segments.title: Opening: Overview of Daf 109 and the Distinction between Kablan and Choker
Mishnah: Choker Renting for Short Period Cannot Plant Flax; Restricted from Sycamore Wood Rights
Seven-Year Rental: Permission to Plant Flax and Cut Sycamore in First Year

macro_segments.title: Amoraim Dispute on Sycamore Overgrowth (Shevach): Abaye vs. Rava

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Position: Renter Receives Payment for Excess Sycamore Growth (Shevach)
Rava's Counter-Position: Renter Gets Nothing for Excess Growth
First Attempt to Refute Rava via Baraita on Vegetables (Yarak)
Refinement: Vegetables Not Yet Mature or Marketable Cannot Be Evaluated
Second Attempt via Shvi'it (Sabbatical Year) — Rejected as Ineffective Proof
Third Attempt via Yovel (Jubilee Year) — Proof That Shevach Must Be Paid
Resolution: Yovel Is Unique — Cannot Prove General Shevach Rule from Yovel Alone

macro_segments.title: Case Law: Rav Pappa's Alfalfa Field and Unexpected Tree Growth

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa Rents Land for Alfalfa; Trees Sprout Unexpectedly
Rav Pappa Claims Shevach; Rav Shisha Challenges with Hypothetical Palm Trees
Rav Pappa's Distinction: Alfalfa Short-Term; These Trees Imply Long-Term Ownership Expectations
Saffron Example: Renter Admits Short-Term Plants; Only Gets Firewood Value
Rav Bibi's Hedge Case: No Loss from Unwanted Plants Around Border

macro_segments.title: The Shatla (Planter) and Rights to Shevach: Multiple Inheritance and Conditional Agreements

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's Shatla Dies; Five Sons-in-Law Inherit — Rav Yosef Disputes Multiple Inheritors
Rav Yosef Offers: Take the Shevach and Leave; or Leave with Nothing
Citation of Rav Yehuda's Rule (Disputed): Inheritors Must Leave Without Shevach
Clarification: Rav Yehuda Never Made That Absolute Claim; Rav Yosef Exaggerated to Intimidate

macro_segments.title: Conditional Agreements: If I Cause Loss, I Forfeit All Compensation (Asmachta Analysis)

micro_segments.title: Shatla Condition: If I Cause Damage, I Leave Without Shevach
Rav Yehuda vs. Rav Kahana Dispute: Does Renter Lose Shevach or Base Wages?
Rabba's Principle: Asmachta (Verbal Condition) Is Unenforceable; Worker Still Gets Base Wages
Comparison to Mishna: Damage Deducted from Wages, Not Entire Forfeiture

macro_segments.title: Case: Runya the Planter Fired Without Notice; Rabba's At-Will Employment Doctrine

micro_segments.title: Runya Causes Damage and Is Kicked Out; Complains of No Warning
Rabba Rules: No Warning Required; Certain Workers Are At-Will
Rava's General Principle: Workers Whose Loss Cannot Be Recovered Are At-Will

macro_segments.title: Shatla's Early Departure and Shevach Split Among Planter, Tenant (Aris), and Owner

micro_segments.title: Shatla Wants to Leave Early to Go to Israel; Requests His Shevach
Rav Papa bar Shmuel Grants Shevach; Rava Questions: Does He Get All of It?
Rav Papa Corrects: Only Half (Pluga D'Shevach); Must Account for New Aris (Tenant)
Rav Ashi's Objection: If New Aris Is Hired, Owner Loses; Reduce to Quarter (Reva D'Danka = Sixth)
Rav Manyumi's Regional Standard: Shatla Gets Half, Aris Gets Third Normally

macro_segments.title: Mathematical Resolution: Quarter of Total vs. Quarter of Remainder (Fractions Debate)

micro_segments.title: Rav Ashi Suggests: Reva D'Shevach (Quarter of Shevach) = One-Sixth of Whole
Rav Acha's Alternative: Give Aris from Your Half; Keep Remainder for Yourself
Resolving the Difference: Reva Mamash (Full Quarter) Works If Tenant Gets Third of Remainder Only
Visual Proof: Two Allocation Methods Ensuring Owner Never Loses
Rav Ashi's Concession: You Are Clever with Fractions

macro_segments.title: Additional Case: Old Vines (Kufsa Saba) — Half vs. Quarter of Value Upon Withering

micro_segments.title: Old Withered Vines: Renter Takes Half of Their Value; If Destroyed by River, Only Quarter

macro_segments.title: Transition: Mishkon (Mortgage) and Dried Trees — Classification as Pira or Karna

micro_segments.title: Mortgage Case: Field Pledged for 10 Years Dries Out After 5 Years
Abaye: Dried Trees Are Pira (Produce); May Be Taken as Firewood Value
Rava: Dried Trees Are Karna (Principal); Cannot Be Taken; Proceeds Buy New Land
Baraita on Cut or Withered Trees: Neither Party May Consume; Must Sell as Firewood, Buy New Land
Dispute Resolution: Is Withering in Normal Time (Like Aging) or Abnormal? Impacts Abaye vs. Rava

topical_tags.term: Reva D'Danka (Quarter of Remainder = Sixth of Whole)
